title: santalum insulare var. insulare
intro: Santalum insulare var. insulare, also known as the Lord Howe Island Sandalwood, is a rare and endemic plant species native to the Lord Howe Island in Australia. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Santalum insulare var. insulare include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce pain and inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as an essential oil, tea, or tincture through various herbal preparations, such as steam distillation and infusion.
